evals.prompt: I've got a system where the React frontend calls 6 different Python microservices directly over REST, and all of them share the same PostgreSQL database. It's getting really slow and hard to deploy. Can you review this architecture?
evals.expected_output: Scorecard identifying Distributed Monolith anti-pattern (shared DB), Chatty Microservices anti-pattern (direct frontend-to-service calls), recommendations for API Gateway pattern, database-per-service strategy, and an ADR for event-driven migration.

assertions.name: identifies-distributed-monolith
assertions.description: Response identifies the shared database as a Distributed Monolith anti-pattern
assertions.expected: Flags shared PostgreSQL database across microservices as Distributed Monolith anti-pattern

assertions.name: produces-scorecard
assertions.description: Response includes an Architecture Scorecard table with quality attribute scores
assertions.expected: Markdown table with Scalability, Maintainability, Resilience scores and findings

assertions.name: actionable-recommendations
assertions.description: Recommendations are specific and actionable, not generic
assertions.expected: Each recommendation includes what, why, and how to fix — not just 'add caching'

assertions.name: rag-module-not-triggered
assertions.description: Response does not invoke Gen AI / RAG review module for a non-AI system
assertions.expected: RAG-specific checklist is not included in the output

evals.prompt: Here's my RAG pipeline for a healthcare Q&A bot at my company: I chunk patient documents at 512 fixed tokens, embed them with text-embedding-3-small, store in Pinecone, retrieve top-3, stuff into a GPT-4o prompt and return the answer directly to clinicians. Can you review this?
evals.expected_output: RAG pipeline checklist evaluation flagging fixed-size chunking, missing re-ranking, no hallucination mitigation, missing Responsible AI layer for healthcare context, no RAGAS evaluation. Critical flags for prompt injection risk and missing human-in-the-loop for clinical decisions.

assertions.name: rag-checklist-triggered
assertions.description: Response uses the Gen AI / RAG review module and checklist
assertions.expected: RAG pipeline checklist is evaluated with chunking, embedding, retrieval, prompt, response sections

assertions.name: responsible-ai-flagged
assertions.description: Response flags missing Responsible AI layer as critical for healthcare context
assertions.expected: 🔴 Critical finding for missing content filtering, audit logging, or human-in-the-loop in clinical context

assertions.name: hallucination-mitigation-flagged
assertions.description: Response identifies missing hallucination mitigation as a risk
assertions.expected: Flags no grounding check, citation tracking, or confidence thresholds

assertions.name: chunking-anti-pattern
assertions.description: Response identifies fixed-size chunking as Naive Chunking anti-pattern
assertions.expected: Recommends semantic chunking with overlap and boundary-aware splitting

evals.prompt: My team is deciding between Kafka and Azure Service Bus for our event-driven order processing system. We're on Azure, team of 8 engineers, and we expect ~50k events/day with occasional spikes to 500k. Can you generate an ADR for this decision?
evals.expected_output: Full ADR document with context, decision (Azure Service Bus recommended for Azure-native team at this scale), consequences including positive and trade-offs, alternatives comparison table between Kafka and Azure Service Bus across latency, ops complexity, cost, and Azure integration.

assertions.name: adr-format-correct
assertions.description: Output follows the ADR template with all required sections
assertions.expected: ADR includes Status, Context, Decision, Consequences (positive + trade-offs + risks), and Alternatives Considered table

assertions.name: context-specific-recommendation
assertions.description: ADR recommendation is tailored to the Azure context and team size, not generic
assertions.expected: Decision accounts for Azure-native deployment, 8-engineer team operational burden, and 50k-500k event volume

assertions.name: alternatives-table
assertions.description: Alternatives section includes a comparison table, not just prose
assertions.expected: Markdown table comparing Kafka vs Azure Service Bus on multiple dimensions
